detail: Weekly research (sonar-deep-research) fabricated source URL and claim. Item: "First ReArm Europe EDIP contracts signed for artillery shells" with source_url https://www.euractiv.com/section/defence/news/eu-unveils-first-rearm-europe-contracts-for-joint-ammo-production/ — URL does not exist (returns Cloudflare challenge/403). Claim conflates March 2024 ASAP €500M allocation with March 2026 EDIP €1.5B programme launch. No such Rheinmetall-Nexter EDIP contract exists. Hallucination propagated: weekly-research → reasoner → synthesiser → publisher → live brief. No pipeline stage validated source URL reachability or cross-checked claim dates against coverage window. Published as ESA lead signal W/E 16 April 2026.
errors: Fabricated source_url: euractiv.com article does not exist
Conflated 2024 ASAP programme with 2026 EDIP programme
Invented Rheinmetall-Nexter contract that never happened
No URL reachability check at any pipeline stage
No date cross-validation between claim date and source publication date
Reasoner accepted fabricated claim as key_evidence without verification
Synthesiser built lead_signal around unverified reasoner claim
Publisher had no factual accuracy gate
resolution: Corrected 2026-04-16: 4 commits to asym-intel-main (9199e1fd, daacbc40, fd8acef6, 12cb854a) correcting report-latest.json, report-2026-04-16.json, weekly-brief.md, report-latest.md. Cloudflare cache purged. R1-R5 guardrails deployed engine-wide (28 commits across pipeline + renderer + Hugo). Source Surfacing Principle added to ENGINE-RULES.md §14. Epistemic dashboard + public transparency page deployed.

summary: Ramparts Issue 4 thin-output bug — class fix shipped
detail: Original Ramparts publisher hand-rolled HTML from AIM canonical and produced ~2.5KB skeletons (page 22851). Root cause: publisher had no understanding of AIM module shape; emitted minimal scaffolding for any unrecognised key. Class fix: thin-frontend adapter pattern (PR #69, merged 0ae2410). Adapter projects canonical → ramparts-v2 shape; Ramparts repo's existing 150KB Node renderer consumes the shaped JSON; publisher only orchestrates and POSTs to WP. Now produces 48,775-char rich WP block.

summary: WP_APP_PASS leaked in plain text in workflow logs
detail: Run 24654557432 of ramparts-publisher.yml printed WP_APP_PASS=RHD2 dBK6 Ik8a pP9d PbRQ evsa via the explicit env re-declare pattern (WP_APP_PASS: ${{ env.WP_APP_PASS }}), which surfaced the secret in step env dumps. Containment: (1) credential rotated in WP admin 2026-04-20; (2) platform-config.md updated (asym-intel-internal commits b15618e, 2c230e1); (3) workflow now ::add-mask:: WP_APP_PASS before $GITHUB_ENV write; (4) explicit re-declare removed from Run Ramparts Publisher step. Both fixes in PR #69.

summary: Ramparts WP REST 401 on first post-rotation publish
detail: ramparts-publisher run 24657843180 reached Step 3 with 48,775-char rich HTML, but all WP REST writes returned 401 Unauthorized for pages 22851, 22643, 22648, 22647, 22649, 22651. Standalone probe of /wp-json/wp/v2/users/me with (asym-intel-engine, kOm6 difp 3LYB yeQa ffYM Ebsl) — both spaced and unspaced — returned 401 rest_not_logged_in. Pipeline is correct; credential pair is wrong. Likely cause: WP_USER spelling or app password regenerated. Pending Peter verification in WP admin (Users → Application Passwords). When pair is correct, re-run with: `gh workflow run ramparts-publisher.yml -f backfill=true` (Issue 4 will re-publish; Step 0 dup-check skipped because slug 2026-04-17 was removed from asym-intel/Ramparts:data/archive.json in commit c114992).
next_action: Peter: confirm WP user that owns app password kOm6...Ebsl; update platform-config.md if needed; re-run workflow.

summary: RESOLVED — WP REST 401 was credential mismatch (app pwd belongs to peterhowitt@gmail.com, not asym-intel-engine/-pipeline)
detail: Peter rotated app pwd a third time, this time noting the misnamed user 'aysm-intel-pipeline' (typo) and creating new 'asym-intel-pipeline'. Direct probe of /wp-json/wp/v2/users/me revealed the new app password W6V9 C3nr 9ogf snGu RR46 Di34 was actually issued under peterhowitt@gmail.com (HTTP 200, returned id 9695148). platform-config.md updated with WP_USER=peterhowitt@gmail.com + new app pwd (asym-intel-internal commit 090494e). Run 24658267317 succeeded end-to-end: page 22851 + 5 standing pages all updated; live page is now 151,209 bytes (was ~2.5KB thin). Adapter pipeline verified working in production.
lesson: Application passwords in WP are bound to specific users. Probe /wp-json/wp/v2/users/me before assuming the username. Trying multiple (user, pw) pairs takes 5 seconds and is cheaper than re-running the workflow.
